mission.title: grpc: A solution to build Apple Silicon (M1) Python binary wheels
mission.description: A solution to build Apple Silicon (M1) Python binary wheels. This issue affects 37+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Check your build output and test logs for errors related to this issue.

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
Currently, the `grpcio` Python package does not distribute functioning binary wheels for the Apple Silicon (M1 processor family) architecture.

steps.title: Apply the fix for A solution to build Apple Silicon (M1) Python binary wheels
steps.description:
While  is 💯 % right and I can't wait to get a pre-built binary for arm64 arch -- the workaround for those looking for one: 

```
pip install --ignore-installed --no-binary grpcio grpcio
```

My team and I use Poetry and I first have to create the env and manually do this command every time,
```yaml
version: 2.1

jobs:
  build-grpcio-apple-silicon-wheel:
    working_directory: ~/grpcio-wheels
    macos:
      xcode: << parameters.xcode_version >>

    parameters:
      python_build_version:
        type: string
        description: The Python version to build the wheel for
      xcode_version:
        type: string
        description: The Xcode version for the CircleCI executor
        default: 13.3.0

    environment:
      CIBW_ARCHS_MACOS: "arm64"
      CIBW_TEST_SKIP: "*_arm64"
      CIBW_BUILD: "<< parameters.python_build_version >>-macosx_arm64"
      CIBW_BUILD_FRONTEND: pip
      
```

steps.title: Confirm A solution to build Apple Silicon (M1) Python… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
While  is 💯 % right and I can't wait to get a pre-built binary for arm64 arch -- the workaround for those looking for one: 

```
pip install --ignore-installed --no-binary grpcio grpcio
```

My team and I use Poetry and I first have to create the env and manually do this command every time, however, it is a workaround that allows our Py310 interpreter to build from source and have a functioning
codeSnippets:
version: 2.1

jobs:
  build-grpcio-apple-silicon-wheel:
    working_directory: ~/grpcio-wheels
    macos:
      xcode: << parameters.xcode_version >>

    parameters:
      python_build_version:
        type: string
        description: The Python version to build the wheel for
      xcode_version:
        type: string
        description: The Xcode version for the CircleCI executor
        default: 13.3.0

    environment:
      CIBW_ARCHS_MACOS: "arm64"
      CIBW_TEST_SKIP: "*_arm64"
      CIBW_BUILD: "<< parameters.python_build_version >>-macosx_arm64"
      CIBW_BUILD_FRONTEND: pip
      CIBW_ENVIRONMENT_MACOS: "GRPC_PYTHON_BUILD_WITH_CYTHON=1"
      CIBW_BEFORE_BUILD: "pip install -r requirements.txt"
    steps:
      - run:
          name: Install cibuildwheel
          command: |

prerequisites.description: gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
